title: Prefer java.nio.Files#delete over java.io.File#delete for better error handling
description: When java.io.File#delete fails, it simply returns false with no indication of the cause. On the other hand, when java.nio.file.Files#delete fails, it returns one of a series of exception types to better indicate the cause of the failure. And since more information is generally better in a debugging situation, java.nio.file.Files#delete is the preferred option.
